subject: [PATCH] mm/migrate_device: pin large folios before splitting

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary:
Reviewer David questioned the necessity of the comment explaining why a temporary reference is taken before dropping the PTE lock, suggesting it's a common mechanism and not unique to this code.

Gave Acked-by
sentiment_signals: questioning the purpose of a comment
reviews.raw_body:
Do we really need that comment (same below?). It's a common mechanism when
keeping to refer to folios after dropping the PTL.

reviews.author: Zi Yan
reviews.summary: Reviewer Zi Yan approved the patch and gave a Reviewed-by tag, indicating they reviewed it thoroughly.
reviews.raw_body:
LGTM. Like David said, the comments might not be needed. Thanks.

Reviewed-by: Zi Yan <ziy@nvidia.com>

Best Regards,
Yan, Zi

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: Reviewer Andrew Morton praised the patch's comments for being helpful in educating new developers about the code, and suggested that they be kept as a way to introduce newcomers to the subject.
reviews.raw_body:
I like the comments!  They may be uninteresting to those who are
already familiar with these things, but they aren't the target audience.

How are others to become familiar, if not by this?

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary: Reviewer David pointed out that dropping the PTE lock before calling migrate_vma_split_folio() can lead to a stale folio pointer, and suggested taking a temporary reference on the folio before dropping the lock.
reviews.raw_body:
I mean, it's one of the basic rules: if you lookup a page in the page table, the
moment you drop the lock that might be invalid.

If we were to document that everywhere... this is not really the secret sauce we
want to document everywhere.

-- 
Cheers,

David

reviews.author: Zi Yan
reviews.summary: Reviewer Yan noted that the patch does not follow established kernel coding conventions for handling large folios in migrate_vma_split_folio(), as it should have taken an elevated reference to the folio before dropping the PTE lock, but instead relies on the split helper consuming a temporary reference.
reviews.raw_body:
In this particular case, split_folio() is used in migrate_vma_split_folio()
and split_folio() wraps __split_huge_page_to_list_to_order(), which documents
the requirement of elevated folio reference. One who wants to get familiar
with kernel code can be expected to chase down to
__split_huge_page_to_list_to_order() and find the requirement.

With LLMs, it is probably as easy as asking \u201cwhy is folio_get() needed here?\u201d.

Best Regards,
Yan, Zi

reviews.author: SJ Park
reviews.summary:
Reviewer questioned whether the Reported-by tag was correctly attributed to a bot, suggesting that the patch author might have read and responded to the review directly rather than through the bot.

Reviewer was confused about the location of a specific comment in the patch series, and provided a link to Sashiko.dev that allows pointing to a specific reply.

Gave Reviewed-by

Reviewer suggested separating recurring code into a separate function and adding the comment only once there, or creating a hotfix version without the comment to be refactored later

Reviewer noted that the patch does not handle the case where a large folio is split concurrently, and suggested taking a temporary reference to the folio before dropping the PTE lock in migrate_vma_collect_pmd().
sentiment_signals: concern about concurrent splitting of large folios
reviews.raw_body:
Just curious.  Has the bot sent the review directly to you?  Or, you read it
from sashiko.dev web site but kindly giving the credit to the bot?

---

The link shows the comments for the entire series, so I was little bit confused
where the real finding is.  Seems like that is on the reply to the patch 5?
And today I learned Sashiko.dev provides a way to point a reply to a specific
patch, like this:

https://sashiko.dev/#/patchset/20260630164143.1595669-1-usama.arif%40linux.dev?part=5

---

I don't mind having the above comments.  Nonetheless, having same content but
different wrapping look special to me.

If people really hate having two (incompletely) duplicated comments, what about
separating the recurring code out and adding the coment once on the function?
Maybe having a hotfix version without the comment and later refactoring with
the function can also be an option?  Just a pure loud thinking.

Anyway, thank you for making Linux safer! :)

---

Thanks,
SJ

2026-07-01.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the device migration code where large folios were not properly pinned before being split. The issue occurred when migrate_vma_collect_pmd() detected a large folio while holding the PTE lock, then dropped the lock before calling migrate_vma_split_folio(). This allowed concurrent reclaim or truncation to replace or clear the entry and drop the last reference to the folio. To fix this, the patch takes a temporary reference on the folio before dropping the PTE lock and passes that reference into migrate_vma_split_folio(), ensuring that the split helper sees only the expected caller pin and preventing potential failures.

reviews.author: Alistair Popple
reviews.summary: The reviewer pointed out that the folio_get() reference is not being dropped or returned anywhere in the code, which can lead to spurious refcounts and make it difficult to track where the matching get/puts occur.
reviews.raw_body:
The interesting part of the comment isn't why the folio_get() is needed, it's
where the corresponding reference is dropped/returned. I've spent far too many
hours in this code trying to figure out why a page didn't migrate because of
a spurious refcount and it's not easy figuring out where the matching get/puts
occur.

So the comment could probably be condensed but I think it's valuable.

 - Alistair

reviews.author: Lance Yang
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Lance Yang noted that getting a reference to the folio after dropping the PTE lock may be overly optimistic, suggesting that it's better to get the reference before dropping the lock.

Gave Reviewed-by
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah, that's how it should have been:

lookup under PTL -> get folio ref -> drop PTL.

Getting a ref after dropping PTL feels a bit too optimistic ...

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary:
Author is addressing feedback about the need to condense comments, specifically in the migrate_vma_split_folio() function. They are open to applying a fixlet or sending a v2 patch if it's easier.

The author is addressing a comment about condensing the comment about folio reference in the code, and has provided a fix-up patch that simplifies the comment.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Andrew!

Could you apply the below fixlet to condense the comment as per Davids', Alistairs' and Zis'
suggestion? I can send a v2 if its easier as well. Thanks!

---

From: Usama Arif <usama.arif@linux.dev>
Date: Wed, 1 Jul 2026 11:12:54 -0700
Subject: [fixup] condense comment about folio reference

Signed-off-by: Usama Arif <usama.arif@linux.dev>
---
 mm/migrate_device.c | 12 ++----------
 1 file changed, 2 insertions(+), 10 deletions(-)

diff --git a/mm/migrate_device.c b/mm/migrate_device.c
index f5a5f699e98e..052167f9ad54 100644
--- a/mm/migrate_device.c
+++ b/mm/migrate_device.c
@@ -311,11 +311,7 @@ static int migrate_vma_collect_pmd(pmd_t *pmdp,
 			if (folio_test_large(folio)) {
 				int ret;
 
-				/*
-				 * Keep the folio stable after dropping the PTE
-				 * lock. migrate_vma_split_folio() consumes this
-				 * reference.
-				 */
+				/* migrate_vma_split_folio() consumes this reference */
 				if (folio != fault_folio)
 					folio_get(folio);
 				lazy_mmu_mode_disable();
@@ -361,11 +357,7 @@ static int migrate_vma_collect_pmd(pmd_t *pmdp,
 			if (folio && folio_test_large(folio)) {
 				int ret;
 
-				/*
-				 * Keep the folio stable after dropping the
-				 * PTE lock. migrate_vma_split_folio() consumes
-				 * this reference.
-				 */
+				/* migrate_vma_split_folio() consumes this reference */
 				if (folio != fault_folio)
 					folio_get(folio);
 				lazy_mmu_mode_disable();
-- 
2.53.0-Meta

2026-07-02.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the device migration code where large folios were not properly pinned before being split. The issue occurred when migrate_vma_collect_pmd() detected a large folio while holding the PTE lock, then dropped the lock before calling migrate_vma_split_folio(). This allowed concurrent reclaim or truncation to replace or clear the entry and drop the last reference to the folio. To fix this, the patch takes a temporary reference on the folio before dropping the PTE lock and passes that reference into migrate_vma_split_folio(), ensuring that the split helper sees only the expected caller pin and preventing potential failures.
